channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Middle East Conflict Alters 2026 Oil Outlook, Oversupply Looms in 2027
item.description: The ongoing conflict in the Middle East has significantly impacted global oil markets, altering forecasts for 2026 and 2027. According to a Reuters poll of analysts, the war has deepened predictions o...

item.title: Magesi goalkeeper commits to Motsepe Foundation Championship push for Premiership return
item.description: Zimbabwean international Elvis Chipezeze has pledged his loyalty to Magesi as the club adapts to life in the Motsepe Foundation Championship following their relegation from the Betway Premiership. The...

item.title: Crest White Brilliance Kit Promoted in July 2026 Sale
item.description: The article highlights the sale of Crest White Brilliance toothpaste, which is being offered at a discounted price ($10 instead of $15) as part of a promotional campaign. It emphasizes customer review...

item.title: Prince George marks 13th birthday with coastal video and prepares for secondary school
item.description: A new video of Prince George, released by Kensington Palace, celebrates his 13th birthday with footage of him enjoying coastal activities in Cornwall. The clip, shot during Easter, features the prince...

item.title: Kim Chiu to Perform at ASAP XP Australia 2026
item.description: Kim Chiu, known for her versatility in the entertainment industry, has confirmed her participation in the ASAP XP Australia 2026 event. The event, which is a major platform for emerging talents, will ...

item.title: Otedola increases stake in FirstHoldCo with N77.6 billion share purchase
item.description: Femi Otedola, chairman of FirstHoldCo Plc, has acquired 706.1 million new shares in the banking group, raising his stake to 21.95%. The purchase, valued at N77.6 billion, was made through his special ...

item.title: Willem Dafoe's 1980s Career: From Experimental Theater to Academy Award Nominations
item.description: Willem Dafoe rose to prominence in the 1980s as a striking character actor known for his intense presence and unique features. His career began with experimental theater in New York, where he became p...

item.title: Universities urge students to prioritize studies over protests amid NEET-UG exam controversy
item.description: The Association of Indian Universities (AIU) has advised higher education institutions to counsel students to focus on academics rather than participating in protests related to recent examination con...

item.title: Ugandan Women's Struggles and Aspirations in Middle East Domestic Work
item.description: Ugandan women are migrating to the Middle East for domestic work, facing abuse and exploitation, but they see it as a way to gain independence. Despite risks like physical, sexual, and psychological a...

item.title: Louvre Reopens Apollo Gallery After Security Enhancements Following Heist
item.description: The Louvre Museum in Paris officially reopened its Apollo Gallery to the public on Wednesday, marking the first time since a high-profile $100-million heist last year. The gallery, one of the museum’s...

item.title: Flying Eagles Begin Title Defence Against Ghana in Yamoussoukro
item.description: Nigeria's U20 football team, the Flying Eagles, are set to kick off their WAFU B title defence against Ghana's Black Satellites in Yamoussoukro, Côte d'Ivoire. The match, scheduled for July 27, 2026, ...

item.title: Eight Strategies to Reduce Ultra-Processed Foods in Family Diets
item.description: The article outlines eight practical strategies for reducing the consumption of ultra-processed foods (UPF) within families, emphasizing long-term consistency and systemic changes. Key approaches incl...

item.title: Andrea Johnson admits legal mandate error in corruption probe
item.description: The Investigating Directorate Against Corruption (Idac) head, Andrea Johnson, admitted to an error in determining whether the corruption case against police crime intelligence head Lt-Gen Dumisani Khu...

item.title: Mine Trade Pledges to Support Tanzanian Mining Sector
item.description: The article discusses Mine Trade's commitment to include Tanzanians in the mining sector, emphasizing efforts to create opportunities for local involvement. The initiative aims to foster economic grow...

item.title: Elon Musk's AI-Driven 'Odyssey' Project Faces Criticism Amid Nolan's Adaptation Success
item.description: Elon Musk has announced plans to create an AI-generated film adaptation of Homer's 'Odyssey,' claiming it will be 'historically accurate' and 'true to the art of Homer.' The project, spearheaded by hi...

item.title: Kenya Government Reassures Burundi Nationals After Viral Incident
item.description: A viral video showing a Kenyan man confronting a Burundian tea and mandazi vendor sparked public outrage, with many calling for the aggressor's arrest. The incident, described as xenophobic by observe...

item.title: Senate impeachment court orders ex-Landbank managers to testify in Sara Duterte's trial
item.description: The Senate impeachment court has approved subpoenas for two former Land Bank of the Philippines branch managers, Violeta Constantino and Nenita Camposano, to appear in the trial of Vice President Sara...

item.title: US Treasury Secretary Commends Pakistan's Economic Reforms and Path to Capital Markets
item.description: The US Treasury Secretary, Scott Bessent, has praised Pakistan's progress in implementing economic reforms and its efforts to re-enter international capital markets. During a meeting with Finance Mini...

item.title: Federal judge allows appellate review of Trump's Jan. 6 tweet immunity claim
item.description: A federal judge has certified for appellate review whether President Donald Trump's January 6, 2021 tweet about the Capitol riot was protected by presidential immunity. U.S. District Judge Amit Mehta ...

item.title: Indonesia Awards 12 Tanzanians with Master's Scholarships via Global Program
item.description: Indonesia has honored 12 Tanzanians with Master's degrees through a global scholarship initiative, marking a significant step in educational collaboration between the two nations. The event, held on J...
